steps.title: Rewrite the radical using fractional exponents
steps.content:
A radical like $\sqrt{x^{153}}$ can be rewritten using a fractional exponent. When we have a square root, we use a denominator of 2 in the exponent:
$$\sqrt{x^{153}} = (x^{153})^{1/2}$$

steps.title: Apply the power rule for exponents
steps.content:
When we have nested exponents like $(x^{153})^{1/2}$, we multiply the exponents together:
$$(x^{153})^{1/2} = x^{153 \cdot \frac{1}{2}} = x^{\frac{153}{2}}$$
This matches choice B — B is correct.
